task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles the edge case of an empty list, but could be more concise
trace: To accomplish this task, I sorted the list in descending order using the `sort()` method with `reverse=True`. This ensures that the largest numbers are at the start of the list. Then, I checked if there were fewer than two distinct values in the list by converting it to a set and checking its length. If there were less than two distinct values, I returned None. Otherwise, I returned the second element (index 1) from the sorted list, which is the second largest number. The edge case I handled is when there are less than two distinct numbers in the input list.
